\begin{document}
% ref: def_3_15
% title: CollidersAndNon
\def\rowref{def\_3\_15}
\phantomsection\label{def_3_15}

\begin{Def}[Colliders and non-colliders]\label{def:collider_noncollider}
    Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def \ref{def-cdmg}); throughout we use the notation of def \ref{not-cdmg} and the edge classifications of def \ref{def-edge-relations}. Let
    \[
        \pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n)
    \]
    be a walk in $G$ (def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.) for some integer $n \ge 0$, with $v_0, \dots, v_n \in J \cup V$ and $a_0, \dots, a_{n-1} \in E \cup L$ satisfying the walk constraint at every $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n - 1\}$:
    \[
        \bigl(a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \;\land\; a_k \in E \cup L\bigr)
        \;\lor\;
        \bigl(a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \;\land\; a_k \in E\bigr).
    \]

    \emph{Arrowhead count at a position.}
    For a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$, the \emph{arrowhead count at $v_k$ on $\pi$}, denoted $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$, is the cardinality
    \[
        \mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)
            := \Bigl|\Bigl\{\, i \in \{k - 1, k\} \,\Bigm|\, 0 \le i \le n - 1 \,\land\, a_i \text{ is an edge into } v_k \text{ in } G \,\Bigr\}\Bigr|,
    \]
    where ``$a_i$ is an edge into $v_k$ in $G$'' is in the sense of def \ref{def-edge-relations}, item~ii.; spelled out, for any $i \in \{0, 1, \dots, n - 1\}$ writing $a_i = (e_1, e_2)$,
    \[
        a_i \text{ is an edge into } v_k
        \quad\iff\quad
        \bigl(a_i \in E \;\land\; e_2 = v_k\bigr)
            \;\lor\;
        \bigl(a_i \in L \;\land\; (e_1 = v_k \,\lor\, e_2 = v_k)\bigr).
    \]
    The index set defining $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ ranges only over the (at most two) walk-incident edges at position $k$, namely $a_{k-1}$ (when $k \ge 1$) and $a_k$ (when $k \le n - 1$); edges of $G$ that are not incident to $v_k$ along $\pi$ are excluded by construction. At an end-position $k \in \{0, n\}$ at most one such index is admissible, so $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) \le 1$ automatically (and $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(0) = 0$ on the trivial walk $n = 0$, where neither index is admissible). At an interior position $1 \le k \le n - 1$ both indices are admissible, so $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) \in \{0, 1, 2\}$. (The stored-pair test ``$a_i$ is an edge into $v_k$'' above is ambiguous in two corner configurations admitted by def \ref{def-cdmg}: at a walk-incident directed self-loop $a_i \in E$ with $v_i = v_{i+1}$, where its $E$-clause fires unconditionally at both adjacent positions $k \in \{i, i + 1\}$; and at a walk-incident writing-mirror walk-step whose underlying stored pair sits simultaneously in $E$ and in $L$, where its $L$-clause fires unconditionally at both adjacent positions regardless of which channel the walk-step actually traverses. The canonical disambiguation at such positions is the side-aware reading of the addition tag \texttt{[collider\_side\_aware\_walkstep\_predicates]}, spelled out in the Encoding note and Treatment of directed self-loops and writing-mirror walk-steps paragraphs below.)

    \emph{Encoding note: side-aware reading via a typed walk-step representation.}
    At a walk-step $a_i$ whose endpoints are distinct ($v_i \ne v_{i+1}$), the walk constraint of def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.\ is typically satisfied by a unique channel assignment: the underlying stored pair lives in exactly one of $E$, $L$, in exactly one orientation, and only one walk-constraint disjunct fires. But def \ref{def-cdmg} admits \emph{writing-mirror} pairs: the irreflexivity restriction applies to $L$ only, and no graph-theoretic mutual-exclusion is imposed between the directed channel $E$ and the bidirected channel $L$, so a single underlying vertex pair $\{u, v\}$ may simultaneously support a directed edge $(u, v) \in E$ (or $(v, u) \in E$) \emph{and} a bidirected edge $s(u, v) \in L$. At a walk-step traversing such a writing-mirror pair, more than one channel assignment to $a_i$ satisfies the walk constraint, and the literal stored-pair ``$a_i$ is an edge into $v_k$'' test of def \ref{def-edge-relations}, item~ii.\ fires its $L$-clause at both adjacent positions $k \in \{i, i+1\}$ irrespective of which channel the walk-step actually traverses. The same kind of literal-test ambiguity arises at a walk-incident directed self-loop $a_i \in E$ with $v_i = v_{i+1} = v$, where both walk-constraint disjuncts apply syntactically and the stored-pair test's $E$-clause fires unconditionally at both adjacent positions.

    The side-aware reading introduced by the addition tag \texttt{[collider\_side\_aware\_walkstep\_predicates]} resolves both corners by committing to the walk-step's recorded traversal channel as the \emph{sole} signal for which channel contributes to the arrowhead test at this step. Each walk-step $a_i$ on $\pi$ carries an explicit channel tag $c_i \in \{\textsf{forward-}E, \textsf{backward-}E, \textsf{bidir-}L\}$, recorded as part of $\pi$ at any walk-step where the underlying vertex/edge sequence alone would leave the channel choice ambiguous -- writing-mirror walk-steps, and directed self-loops where both orientations $v_i \to v_{i+1}$ and $v_{i+1} \to v_i$ satisfy the walk constraint; at all other walk-steps the channel is forced by the walk's data. The per-step contribution of $a_i$ to $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ at each adjacent position $k \in \{i, i+1\}$ is determined \emph{solely} by $c_i$, never consulting whether the underlying stored pair also sits in the opposite channel of $G$:
    \begin{itemize}
        \item $c_i = \textsf{forward-}E$ (underlying $(v_i, v_{i+1}) \in E$, traversed forward $v_i \to v_{i+1}$): contributes $1$ at $k = i + 1$ and $0$ at $k = i$;
        \item $c_i = \textsf{backward-}E$ (underlying $(v_{i+1}, v_i) \in E$, traversed backward $v_{i+1} \to v_i$): contributes $1$ at $k = i$ and $0$ at $k = i + 1$;
        \item $c_i = \textsf{bidir-}L$ (underlying $s(v_i, v_{i+1}) \in L$): contributes $1$ at both adjacent positions $k \in \{i, i+1\}$.
    \end{itemize}
    A directed step ($c_i \in \{\textsf{forward-}E, \textsf{backward-}E\}$) commits to its $E$-channel reading even when a coexisting $L$-edge $s(v_i, v_{i+1}) \in L$ would, under the literal stored-pair test, additionally contribute an arrowhead at the otherwise-zero adjacent position (the writing-mirror case). Symmetrically, a bidirected step ($c_i = \textsf{bidir-}L$) commits to its $L$-channel bidirected reading even when a coexisting $E$-edge is present in $G$. This refinement strictly overrides the literal stored-pair test of def \ref{def-edge-relations}, item~ii.\ at both corner kinds (writing-mirror walk-steps traversed via the $E$-channel, and directed self-loops), and is taken as the canonical reading of $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ used by the collider/non-collider classification below. On walks that traverse neither corner configuration, the side-aware and literal stored-pair readings coincide pointwise.

    The Lean realisation of this definition implements the side-aware reading faithfully via a typed walk-step representation: each walk-step carries a constructor tag -- one of \emph{forward-$E$}, \emph{backward-$E$}, or \emph{bidir-$L$} -- recording the traversal channel directly, alongside typed source/target end indices at the type level. The per-step contribution to $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ is read off the constructor tag and the type-level ends alone, with \emph{no} consultation of the opposite channel of $G$: a forward-$E$ step contributes $1$ at the type-level target end and $0$ at the type-level source end; a backward-$E$ step mirrors, contributing $1$ at the type-level source end and $0$ at the type-level target end; a bidir-$L$ step contributes $1$ at both type-level ends. There is \emph{no} additional opposite-channel $L$-disjunct on the directed branches and \emph{no} additional $E$-disjunct on the bidirected branch -- the constructor tag alone carries the side-aware commitment, exactly realising the per-step contribution formula above. In particular, the strict deviation from the literal stored-pair test is preserved at writing-mirror walk-steps traversed via the $E$-channel as well as at directed self-loops.

    \emph{Classification.}
    The position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ on the walk $\pi$ -- or, by abuse, the node $v_k$ at position $k$ -- is called:
    \begin{enumerate}[label=\roman*.)]
        \item a \emph{non-collider on $\pi$} iff $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) \le 1$;
        \item a \emph{collider on $\pi$} iff $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) = 2$.
    \end{enumerate}
    By construction these two classifications are mutually exclusive and jointly exhaustive over $\{0, 1, \dots, n\}$: every position $k$ on $\pi$ is exactly one of a non-collider or a collider on $\pi$.

    \emph{Reconciliation with the source-block pattern writings.}
    The source block characterises non-colliders by a list of four pattern-cases (end-node, left chain, right chain, fork) glossed as ``at most one arrowhead pointing towards $v_k$'', and colliders by a single pattern-case glossed as ``two arrowheads pointing towards $v_k$ on the walk $\pi$''. Below we reconcile these visual writings with the count-based classification, taking the count-based classification (the LN's ``i.e.''\ gloss) as canonical. The visual writings $v_{k-1} \tuh v_k$, $v_{k-1} \hut v_k$, $v_{k-1} \huh v_k$, $v_{k-1} \suh v_k$, $v_{k-1} \hus v_k$ are unfolded per def \ref{not-cdmg}, items~2--6.
    \begin{itemize}
        \item \textbf{end-node} ($k \in \{0, n\}$): $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) \le 1$ holds automatically, hence the position is a non-collider on $\pi$. (For the trivial walk $n = 0$, the unique position $k = 0 = n$ has $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(0) = 0$ and is a non-collider.)
        \item \textbf{left chain} ($1 \le k \le n - 1$; LN writing $v_{k-1} \hut v_k \hus v_{k+1}$): the walk's $a_{k-1}$ is \emph{not} an edge into $v_k$, while its $a_k$ \emph{is} an edge into $v_k$. Spelled out via the walk constraint, $a_{k-1} = (v_k, v_{k-1}) \in E$ (a directed edge with arrowhead at $v_{k-1}$ and tail at $v_k$), and either $a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \in E$ or $a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \in L$. Count: $0 + 1 = 1$; non-collider.
        \item \textbf{right chain} ($1 \le k \le n - 1$; LN writing $v_{k-1} \suh v_k \tuh v_{k+1}$): the walk's $a_{k-1}$ \emph{is} an edge into $v_k$, while its $a_k$ is \emph{not} an edge into $v_k$. Spelled out, either $a_{k-1} = (v_{k-1}, v_k) \in E$ or $a_{k-1} = (v_{k-1}, v_k) \in L$, and $a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \in E$. Count: $1 + 0 = 1$; non-collider.
        \item \textbf{fork} ($1 \le k \le n - 1$; LN writing $v_{k-1} \hut v_k \tuh v_{k+1}$): neither walk-incident edge is an edge into $v_k$. Spelled out, $a_{k-1} = (v_k, v_{k-1}) \in E$ and $a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \in E$. Count: $0 + 0 = 0$; non-collider.
        \item \textbf{collider} ($1 \le k \le n - 1$; LN writing $v_{k-1} \suh v_k \hus v_{k+1}$): both walk-incident edges $a_{k-1}, a_k$ are edges into $v_k$. Spelled out, $a_{k-1} \in E \cup L$ with right-component $v_k$ if directed (or either component $v_k$ if bidirected), and analogously for $a_k$ with left-component $v_k$ if directed (or either component $v_k$ if bidirected). Count: $1 + 1 = 2$; collider.
    \end{itemize}
    The four non-collider patterns realise exactly the interior counts $\{0, 1\}$, and the collider pattern realises exactly the interior count $2$; together with the end-node case they exhaust the positions of $\pi$. The pattern-cases consult \emph{walk-incident} edges $a_{k-1}, a_k$ only -- arrowheads contributed by edges of $G$ that are not on $\pi$ at $v_k$ are excluded from $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ by construction. (This fixes the scope of the LN's gloss ``at most one (resp.\ two) arrowheads pointing towards $v_k$''.) For walks that traverse a directed self-loop $a_i \in E$ with $v_i = v_{i+1} = v$ -- where the literal pattern writings would otherwise admit any of the four conjunctions above simultaneously -- or a writing-mirror walk-step whose underlying stored pair sits simultaneously in $E$ and in $L$ -- where the literal pattern writings admit both the directed and the bidirected channel interpretations -- the recorded walk-traversal channel of $a_i$ ($\textsf{forward-}E$, $\textsf{backward-}E$, or $\textsf{bidir-}L$) selects exactly one pattern per adjacent position, per the side-aware reading of the addition tag \texttt{[collider\_side\_aware\_walkstep\_predicates]}; see the Treatment of directed self-loops and writing-mirror walk-steps paragraph below for the per-position contribution.

    \emph{Treatment of directed self-loops and writing-mirror walk-steps.}
    The literal stored-pair test ``$a_i$ is an edge into $v_k$'' of def \ref{def-edge-relations}, item~ii.\ is ambiguous in two corner configurations admitted by def \ref{def-cdmg} but not excluded by the walk constraint of def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.:
    \begin{itemize}
        \item \textbf{Directed self-loop walk-steps.} A directed self-loop $(v, v) \in E$ is admitted by def \ref{def-cdmg} (the irreflexivity restriction applies to $L$, not to $E$). At a walk-incident self-loop edge $a_i = (v_i, v_{i+1}) = (v, v) \in E$ with $v_i = v_{i+1} = v$, both walk-constraint disjuncts of def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.\ apply syntactically, and both the LN's literal pattern writings $v_{k-1} \tuh v_k$ and $v_{k-1} \hut v_k$ (def \ref{not-cdmg}, items~2--3) and the stored-pair test collapse to the single condition $(v, v) \in E$, firing unconditionally at both adjacent positions $k \in \{i, i + 1\}$ for which $v_k = v$. The stored-pair count formula above would commit, at such a self-loop traversal, to contribution $1$ from $a_i$ at both adjacent positions, and a single interior position $k$ traversing the self-loop would simultaneously match the ``left chain'', ``right chain'', ``fork'', and ``collider'' pattern conjunctions of the Reconciliation paragraph above.
        \item \textbf{Writing-mirror walk-steps.} At a non-self-loop walk-step ($v_i \ne v_{i+1}$) whose underlying stored pair $(v_i, v_{i+1})$ (or $(v_{i+1}, v_i)$) is in $E$ \emph{and} whose unordered version $s(v_i, v_{i+1})$ is in $L$ -- the writing-mirror configuration permitted by def \ref{def-cdmg}, which does not impose $E \cap L = \emptyset$ -- the walk constraint of def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.\ is satisfied by more than one channel assignment to $a_i$, and the stored-pair test's $L$-clause fires unconditionally at both adjacent positions $k \in \{i, i + 1\}$ regardless of which channel the walk-step actually traverses. The literal pattern writings of def \ref{not-cdmg} similarly admit both the directed and the bidirected channel interpretations at such a walk-step. (The writing-mirror self-loop sibling -- $(v, v) \in E$ and $s(v, v) \in L$ simultaneously -- is structurally impossible by def \ref{def-cdmg}'s \texttt{hL\_irrefl}, which forbids self-pair elements in $L$.)
    \end{itemize}

    The canonical disambiguation in both cases is the \emph{side-aware} reading introduced by the addition tag \texttt{[collider\_side\_aware\_walkstep\_predicates]}, as spelled out in the Encoding note paragraph above: each walk-step $a_i$ on $\pi$ carries an explicit traversal channel $c_i \in \{\textsf{forward-}E, \textsf{backward-}E, \textsf{bidir-}L\}$, with the choice recorded explicitly at any walk-step where the underlying vertex/edge sequence alone would leave the channel ambiguous, and the per-step contribution to $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ is read \emph{solely} off $c_i$. At a directed self-loop $a_i \in E$, the side-aware reading commits the arrowhead contribution to a single adjacent position -- $k = i + 1$ when $c_i = \textsf{forward-}E$, $k = i$ when $c_i = \textsf{backward-}E$ -- and contributes $0$ at the other adjacent position; the $\textsf{bidir-}L$ channel cannot fire at a self-loop because def \ref{def-cdmg}'s irreflexivity restriction on $L$ rules out $s(v, v) \in L$ outright, so the strict side-aware refinement at self-loops is preserved by construction. At a writing-mirror walk-step traversed via $c_i \in \{\textsf{forward-}E, \textsf{backward-}E\}$, the side-aware reading ignores the coexisting $L$-edge entirely, contributing only at the directed channel's arrowhead-receiving end (per the per-step contribution formula in the Encoding note); analogously, a writing-mirror walk-step traversed via $c_i = \textsf{bidir-}L$ commits to the bidirected contribution (arrowheads at both adjacent positions) and ignores the coexisting $E$-edge. This explicitly replaces, at both corner kinds, the literal stored-pair test's reading of $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$; on walks that traverse neither a directed self-loop nor a writing-mirror walk-step the side-aware and stored-pair readings coincide pointwise, so no other part of the definition is affected by the refinement. The recorded walk-traversal channel also selects exactly one of the LN's literal $\tuh / \hut / \huh$ pattern conjunctions per walk-step at each adjacent position, resolving the apparent multi-pattern overlap at both corner kinds.
\end{Def}

\end{document}
